evals.prompt: Run an architecture review (zuvo:architecture --mode review src/) focusing on layering and module dependencies. Tell me about any dependency-direction or cycle problems.
evals.expected_output: The architecture review catches the structural problems in src/: a layering / dependency-direction violation — src/domain/order.ts (the domain layer) imports persist() from src/infra/repository.ts (the infrastructure layer), which inverts the correct dependency direction (infrastructure should depend on the domain, never the reverse) — and the resulting circular dependency between the domain and infra layers (order.ts ↔ repository.ts). Each is cited at its file:line. The review writes its report to zuvo/audits/ and modifies no source file.
assertions: The review records a layering / dependency-direction violation — src/domain/order.ts (domain layer) imports persist() from src/infra/repository.ts (infrastructure layer), inverting the correct infra→domain direction — citing its file:line.
The review records the resulting circular dependency between the domain and infrastructure layers (src/domain/order.ts ↔ src/infra/repository.ts import each other), citing the cycle.
The review writes an architecture review report to zuvo/audits/ recording the layering-violation finding, and it modifies no source file.

evals.prompt: Run an architecture review (zuvo:architecture --mode review src/) focusing on layering and module dependencies. Tell me if the layering is clean.
evals.expected_output: The architecture review shows discipline: it does NOT flag a layering / dependency-direction violation — the domain layer (order.ts) is pure and imports nothing inward, infrastructure (repository.ts) depends INWARD on the domain (the correct direction), and the application layer (checkout-service.ts) composes both — and it does NOT flag a circular dependency, because the module graph is acyclic (app → {domain, infra}, infra → domain). It records the architecture as clean on layering and coupling and writes its report to zuvo/audits/, modifying no source file — no fabricated finding.
assertions: The review records no layering / dependency-direction violation — the domain layer (src/domain/order.ts) is pure and imports nothing inward, and infrastructure (src/infra/repository.ts) correctly depends on the domain (infra→domain direction).
The review records no circular dependency in src/ — the module graph is acyclic (app → domain and infra; infra → domain; domain imports nothing local).

The review records the architecture as clean on layering and coupling — no layering-violation and no circular-dependency finding — and writes its report to zuvo/audits/ without modifying any source file.
